data.title: IQSTEL reports 1H 2025 preliminary revenue of $128.8 million
data.description:
• IQSTEL (NASDAQ:IQST) on Thursday announced 1H 2025 preliminary unaudited revenue of $128.8 million. June alone contributed $27.3 million, a significant increase from May's $23.7 million.
  • Starting July 1st, IQSTEL will begin consolidating revenue from its newly acquired subsidiary, Globetopper, expected to add $5 million to $6 million per month in additional revenue.
  • On this trajectory, IQSTEL expects to reach a $400 million annualized revenue run rate during Q3 — several months ahead of the company's original year-end target. This milestone represents a critical leap forward in the company's strategic roadmap toward achieving its $1 billion revenue goal by 2027.
  • IQST -0.03% premarket to $9.30.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rocket Pharmaceuticals gets FDA RMAT status for heart disorder therapy
data.description:
• Rocket Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RCKT) said that the U.S. FDA has given regenerative medicine advanced therapy (RMAT) status to their gene therapy, RP-A601, for treating a heart condition known as PKP2-arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathy.
  • The stock is up 14% premarket.
  • The RMAT allows the company to have early discussions with the FDA about potential ways to measure the therapy's effectiveness, which could help speed up the approval process.

data.title: Wipro GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line, gross revenue of $2.58B beats by $10M; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Wipro press release (NYSE:WIT): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line.
  • Gross revenue of $2.58B (decrease of 1.6% Q/Q and increase of 0.8% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • IT services segment revenue was at $2,587.4 million, decrease of 0.3% QoQ and 1.5% YoY.
  • Total bookings was at $4,971 million, up by 24.1% QoQ and 50.7% YoY in constant currency. Large deal bookings was at $2,666 million, an increase of 49.7% QoQ and 130.8% YoY in constant currency.
  • Operating cash flows of ₹41.1 billion ($479.6 million), increase of 9.8% QoQ and 2.9% YoY.
  • Declared interim dividend of ₹5 ($0.061) per equity share/ADS.
  • Outlook for the Quarter ending September 30, 2025: "We expect revenue from our IT Services business segment to be in the range of $2,560 million to $2,612 million. This translates to sequential guidance of (-)1.0% to 1.0% in constant currency terms. "

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher buys Equinox Agency
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) has acquired Emmaus, Pennsylvania-based employee health and benefits consulting firm, Equinox Agency.
  • Terms of the deal were not disclosed.

data.title: Plumas Bancorp declares $0.30 dividend
data.description:
• Plumas Bancorp (NASDAQ:PLBC) declares $0.30/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.78%
  • Payable Aug. 15; for shareholders of record Aug. 1; ex-div Aug. 1.
  • See PLBC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Wayfair plans to hold a 'Black Friday in July' event next week
data.description:
Wayfair (NYSE:W) announced the timing of its 2025 Black Friday in July event. The five-day sale will run from July 24 to July 28. 

Under the banner "Black Friday comes early," Wayfair (NYSE:W) is offering shoppers up to 80% off across all home categories, including furniture, appliances, bedding, decor, and more. Early access is available on July 23 via the Wayfair (W) app. The company said the event features a mix of deep sitewide discounts, with daily flash sales and limited-quantity doorbuster deals to further entice shoppers. Wayfair Rewards members can expect extra savings, and the sale will highlight top brands such as Blackstone, Cuisinart, Sealy, and SharkNinja (SN). Most items will ship free, and thousands are marked as Wayfair Verified for quality assurance. 

Wayfair President of Commercial and Operations Jon Blotner said the company’s goal with Black Friday in July is to deliver big and "unbeatable" deals well ahead of the typical November Black Friday. Of course, the retail sector was lit up last week by Amazon Prime Day and corresponding sales events. 

Black Friday in July is still relatively new for Wayfair (W). The inaugural event took place in 2024, running from July 26 to July 29, and featured similarly extensive discounts and exclusive offers for professional members. The online retailer said on the subsequent earnings call that the consumer response was positive.

Shares of Wayfair (W) are on a hot streak, up 18% over the last six weeks.

data.title: AgriFORCE capitalizes on market momentum, expands Alberta mining & treasury strategy
data.description:
• AgriFORCE (NASDAQ:AGRI) Thursday said that it is growing its treasury-denominated BTC holdings, capitalizing on price appreciation to bolster financial resilience.
  • To date, the company has mined 8.67 BTC, representing approximately $1.1 million based on recent all-time high prices.
  • Stablecoin infrastructure: AgriFORCE is also exploring integration of stablecoin on/off ramps-such as USDC and DAI-to streamline digital payments, payroll services, and treasury liquidity, reducing reliance on conventional banking systems, the company said.

  • AgriFORCE (NASDAQ:AGRI) looks to launch stablecoin rails before year-end for B2B and internal use cases, among many other plans.

data.title: Starbucks facing hurdles to meet comparable sales estimates - Jefferies
data.description:
Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) could miss comparable sales estimates in the coming quarters as complex staffing and operational issues might take longer than expected to correct, while necessary and “significant investments” will weigh on earnings.

The sober outlook on Starbucks’ (NASDAQ:SBUX) traffic and valuation led Jefferies’ Andy Barish to downgrade the stock to Underperform from Hold and set a $76 price target that is 18% below Wednesday’s closing price.

“In our view, the stock has surpassed reasonable expectations for improving fundamentals,” Barish writes, citing credit/debit card data, foot traffic and app data, all of which indicate a risk to the consensus estimate for comparable sales of 2.2%.

Barish expects comparable sales trends will be “worse than expected” through 2027 with an uncertain macro environment not helping, albeit with offset possible from improving operations.

As for China, the sector “appears to remain a difficult competitive environment," with most of the large and rapidly growing competitors offering significant value proposition compared to Starbucks. Barish notes that the company’s recent decision to lower prices on dozens of iced drinks to attract more afternoon business will ultimately lead to near zero operating margins for the China business.

“We believe a tough, long turnaround is still ahead given the complexity of the Starbucks size and cultural/operational issues,” Barish says, adding that he believes the 35x “premium” valuation is unwarranted given “overly optimistic” street expectations.

The downgrade is pressuring shares, last traded with a loss of nearly 2% ahead of Thursday’s open.

Wall Street analysts are mostly bullish on Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) with a consensus Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha analysts and Quant rating view Starbucks (SBUX) as a Hold.

data.title: Element 29 announces private placement of up to $3.5M
data.description:
• Element 29 Resources (TSXV:ECU:CA) intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of up to 7M units of the company at a price of $0.50 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of $3.5M.
  • Each unit issuable under the financing consists of one common share in the capital of the company and one-half of one non-transferable common share purchase warrant.
  • The net proceeds from the financing will be used to fund exploration activities at the company's Perú projects, including the Phase-III drill program at its flagship Elida porphyry copper-molybdenum-silver deposit in west-central Perú, and for general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: USB, ABT, GE, NFLX
data.description:
Stock index futures seesawed on Thursday, a day after Wall Street closed in the green after President Donald Trump denied reports of planning to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Here are four stocks to keep an eye on:

U.S. Bancorp (USB) shares were down 4% in premarket trade after the bank issued soft Q3 guidance, following lower-than-expected revenue and net interest income in its Q2 2025. The bank reaffirmed its FY2025 guidance of net revenue growth.

Abbott Laboratories (ABT) stock fell 4.1% before the opening bell after the MedTech  company provided its guidance for the third quarter slightly below consensus following a mixed earnings report for Q2.

GE Aerospace (GE) rose 1.3% in premarket trade after beating estimates for Q2 adjusted earnings and revenues and raising its full-year financial guidance. The stock is poised to exceed the firm's all-time intraday high of $266.86 set in the previous session. For FY 2025, GE (GE) said it now expects adjusted EPS of $5.60-$5.80, up from previous guidance of $5.10-$5.45.

Netflix (NFLX) stock will be under the radar as the company will post its second-quarter results on Thursday; investors would want to see higher pricing actions and an improving advertising market to bump up sales for the streaming giant. The firm is expected to report an EPS of $7.09 on revenue of $11.06 billion.
